zh.lifeSummary: 當George Barne於1525年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，他的父親Sir George Barne II40歲，母親Alice Brookes21歲。他於大約1557年與Anne Garrard在英國英格蘭倫敦結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1586年在英國倫敦被列為haberdasher.  lord mayor of london。他去世於1592年1月2日在英國肯特武爾維其享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 埃德蒙 馮 金 煙雨 聖 尼古拉斯 阿可斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Barne于1525年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，他的父亲Sir George Barne II40岁，母亲Alice Brookes21岁。他于大约1557年与Anne Garrard在英国英格兰倫敦结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1586年在英国倫敦被列为haberdasher.  lord mayor of london。他去世于1592年1月2日在英国肯特武爾維其享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦聖 埃德蒙 馮 金 煙雨 聖 尼古拉斯 阿可斯。

nameMeanings.name: Barne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn. In the Midlands and East Anglia this is based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn, anglicized as Beorn; in the south-west it is from Old English Beorn; both names are from a word meaning ‘warrior’. Some of the following surname bearers may alternatively belong with other senses below.

2 English: status name from Middle English barn (Old Scandinavian barn) ‘child’. This term is found in Domesday Book as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child ( see Child ).

3 English: locative name, nickname for residence near or employment at a barn, Middle English bern (Old English bere-ærn). See also Barnes . Some of the post-medieval bearers may alternatively belong with (1) or (2).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Barn
meanings: Scottish:  see  Barne   and  compare  Berne  .

variants.name: Barner
meanings: English: in southern England a habitational name for someone who lived by a barn, from Middle English berner ‘dweller by the barn’. Elsewhere possibly a variant of Berner .
Scottish: unexplained.
North German: derivative of the ancient Germanic personal name Barnher or Bernher (see Berner 6).

variants.name: Barnes
meanings: English: habitational name from Barnes (on the Surrey bank of the Thames in London), named with Old English bere-ærn ‘barn, a storehouse for barley and other grain’, or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a barn or barns, from Middle English barn ‘barn, granary’.

English: variant of Barne, with excrescent -s, derived from either the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn (based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn or Old English Beorn, both from a word meaning ‘warrior’), or from Middle English barn (Old Norse barn) ‘child’. The latter term is found as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child (see Child ).
Irish: in Ireland in many cases this is no doubt the English name, but in others it is possibly an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Bearáin ‘descendant of Bearán’, a byname meaning ‘spear’.

variants.name: Domesday Book
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn. In the Midlands and East Anglia this is based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn, anglicized as Beorn; in the south-west it is from Old English Beorn; both names are from a word meaning ‘warrior’. Some of the following surname bearers may alternatively belong with other senses below.

2 English: status name from Middle English barn (Old Scandinavian barn) ‘child’. This term is found in Domesday Book as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child ( see Child ).

3 English: locative name, nickname for residence near or employment at a barn, Middle English bern (Old English bere-ærn). See also Barnes . Some of the post-medieval bearers may alternatively belong with (1) or (2).

variants.name: Berne
meanings: French: from a short form of the personal name Bernard or any other name containing the ancient Germanic element ber(n) ‘bear’.
Altered form of Swiss German   Berni  .
English and Scottish: variant of Barne (see Barnes 2) or Burn .

variants.name: Bearne
meanings: Scots: Bearn, a child, a human creature

variants.name: Byrne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Aidan, Liam, Niall, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, John Patrick, Keelin, Kieran, Siobhan.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Broin ‘descendant of Bran’, a personal name based on bran ‘raven’. Bran was the name of a son of the King of Leinster, who died at Cologne in 1052, and also of the hero of an 8th century voyage tale.
Irish: in some cases, possibly from Ó Birn ‘descendant of Biorn’, Irish form of the Old Norse personal name Bjǫrn.

variants.name: Child
meanings: English:

nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When George Barne was born in 1525, in London, Middlesex, England, his father, Sir George Barne II, was 40 and his mother, Alice Brookes, was 21. He married Anne Garrard about 1557, in London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 5 daughters. In 1586, his occupation is listed as haberdasher.  lord mayor of london in London, England. He died on 2 January 1592, in Woolwich, Kent, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in St Edmund the King with St Nicholas Acons, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Barne est né en 1525, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Sir George Barne II, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Alice Brookes, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Anne Garrard environ 1557, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 5 filles. En 1586, sa profession de son indiquée est haberdasher.  lord mayor of london à Londres, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 2 janvier 1592, à Woolwich, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à St Edmund the King with St Nicholas Acons, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als George Barne 1525 in London, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir George Barne II 40 und seine Mutter Alice Brookes 21. Er hat um 1557 in London, England, United Kingdom Anne Garrard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 10 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1586 ist sein Beruf als haberdasher.  lord mayor of london in London, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 2. Januar 1592 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Woolwich, Kent, England und wurde in St Edmund the King with St Nicholas Acons, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George Barne è nato il nel 1525 nel London, Middlesex, England, il suo padre, Sir George Barne II, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Alice Brookes, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Anne Garrard circa 1557, nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 5 figlie. Nel 1586, la sua occupazione è indicata come haberdasher.  lord mayor of london nel London, England. Morì il 2 gennaio 1592, nel Woolwich, Kent, England, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a St Edmund the King with St Nicholas Acons, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: George Barneが1525年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた時，父Sir George Barne IIは40歳，母Alice Brookesは21歳であった。約1557年にイギリスイギリスロンドンでAnne Garrardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。1586年，イギリスロンドンでの職業はhaberdasher.  lord mayor of londonと記録されている。彼は1592年1月2日にイギリスケントウルウィチで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンスト エドムンド ス キング ウィス スト ニチオラス アコンスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: George Barne(이)가 1525년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir George Barne II(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Brookes(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1557년 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 Anne Garrard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1586년, 그의 직업은 영국 런던에서 haberdasher.  lord mayor of london이었다. 그는 1592년 1월 2일 영국 켄트 보위치에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 에드먼드 테 킨그 위스 스트 닉홀아스 악스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George Barne nasceu em 1525, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir George Barne II, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Alice Brookes, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Garrard aproximadamente 1557, em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 5 filhas. Em 1586, sua profissão aparece como haberdasher.  lord mayor of london em London, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 2 de janeiro de 1592, em Woolwich, Kent, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em St Edmund the King with St Nicholas Acons, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Barne родился в 1525 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir George Barne II было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Brookes, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Garrard около 1557 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 5 дочерей. В 1586 году он работал по профессии haberdasher.  lord mayor of london в местности Лондон, Англия. Он умер 2 января 1592 г. в местности вулвич, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности ст эдмунд т кинг вит ст ничолас акунс, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Barne nació en 1525, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir George Barne II, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Alice Brookes, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Anne Garrard aproximadamente 1557, en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 5 hijas. En 1586, su ocupación registrada era haberdasher.  lord mayor of london en London, Inglaterra. Murió el 2 de enero de 1592, en Woolwich, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en St Edmund the King with St Nicholas Acons, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
